title: No warning or error message when `add` command operates on bogus selection
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
read_verilog /path/to/yosys/examples/igloo2/example.v
add -wire foo 1 opksadl sda90sd
select -list
```

## Expected behavior

The `add` command *should* either warn or generate an error about one or more of the following:
1) 'opksadl' and 'sda90sd' are not valid object names
2) attempting to add something to an empty selection
3) command was `-wire` but ultimately no wires were added

## Actual behavior

No such warnings or errors are generated.  Instead, nothing happens.  The selection is not changed, no wire is added, and nothing is printed to the console.
code_blocks:
read_verilog /path/to/yosys/examples/igloo2/example.v
add -wire foo 1 opksadl sda90sd
select -list
